decision_trigger: Complaint acknowledged but no substantive action followed.
recall_question: What happens when acknowledgement is provided without action?
desired_outcome: A substantive response and evidence-based next step

actors.name: Complainant
actors.likely_behaviour: preserve records, follow up, and escalate if necessary

actors.name: Administrative Office
actors.incentive_or_pressure: manage workload and reduce procedural exposure
actors.likely_behaviour: acknowledge receipt but delay substantive engagement

evidence_bundle.label: Acknowledgement email
evidence_bundle.relevance: Shows that receipt was acknowledged without substantive action

deadlines.label: Follow-up threshold
deadlines.consequence: Continued silence strengthens the case for escalation

timeline.label: Complaint submitted
timeline.description: Formal complaint submitted to the institution

timeline.label: Acknowledgement received
timeline.description: Institution acknowledged receipt of the complaint

escalation_paths.name: Written follow-up
escalation_paths.likely_response: A narrow reply or continued delay

linked_cases.note: Earlier matter involving delayed substantive response

structural_insight: Acknowledgement can create the appearance of progress without advancing resolution.
decision_note: Case remains open because acknowledgement did not produce action.
learning_capture: Acknowledgement is not the same as resolution.
